***************************************************************************** THIS DOCUMENT IS OUT OF DATE AND IS HERE FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY Tlearn is no longer installed on the EECS Instructional computers. ******************************
uctional computers. ***************************************************************************** Sep 12 2006 CONTENTS: Tlearn on the Instructional UNIX systems Using the tlearn

the Instructional UNIX systems Using the tlearn software (UNIX) Tlearn installation files (UNIX) Tlearn on the Instructional NT systems Reporting bugs For more information Tlearn on the Instructional UNIX systems -----------------------------

ctional UNIX systems ---------------------------------------- 'tlearn' is available on the Instructional UNIX systems on the Solaris SPARC computers such as pulsar.cs and the SunRay labs on the second floor of Soda Hall. Please see http://ins

econd floor of Soda Hall. Please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/clients for a complete list of login servers and labs. It is located in /share/instsww/tlearn. The programs can be started using these UNIX commands: /share

started using these UNIX commands: /share/instsww/bin/tlearn (command line interface) /share/instsww/bin/xtlearn (X Window version) /share/instsww/bin/tlearn_dsp (CONNEX display-language version) 'tlearn' is a neural network si

guage version) 'tlearn' is a neural network simulation package to accompany the work Elman, J. L. and Plunkett, Kim. Exercises in Rethinking Innateness: A Handbook for Connectionist Simulations. Cambridge: MIT Press, 1997. This softwa

tions. Cambridge: MIT Press, 1997. This software is free (released under the GNU General Public License), and supported by the Instructional group for Prof. Wilensky and CS188. Currently we have only compiled it on Solaris SPARC; the code i

have only compiled it on Solaris SPARC; the code is old and required a little hand-hacking to get going, but should be portable with a little work if the program is required on other architectures. Xtlearn, a self-contained X Window System

es. Xtlearn, a self-contained X Window System interface which provides for the generation of error curves, is also available. Using the tlearn software (UNIX) ------------------------------- You can run the program using the commands s

You can run the program using the commands setenv PATH "${PATH}:/share/n/tlearn/bin:/share/instsww/bin" tlearn "tlearn_dsp", is identical to "tlearn" but also provides for an interface to CONNEX, a language which can display the param

to CONNEX, a language which can display the parameters and results of a connectionist simulation graphically. tlearn_dsp is not really an X Window interface per se, though; if you want to use the X Window interface "xtlearn", use the comm

use the X Window interface "xtlearn", use the command "xtlearn". You have to start xtlearn in the same directory as your .cf, .data, and .teach files, and that directory must be writable by you. Except for the CONNEX input file, tlearn and

. Except for the CONNEX input file, tlearn and tlearn_dsp take the same input files, specified with the -f option. To supply the CONNEX input file, if you are working with a fileroot "xor" (e.g., files named xor.cf, xor.data, xor.teach)

(e.g., files named xor.cf, xor.data, xor.teach), name the CONNEX input file "xor.dsp". "tlearn_dsp" will look for the .dsp extension and bring up a CONNEX window with the results; you can save the picture with tools such as "xwd", and kill t

e the picture with tools such as "xwd", and kill the window when you're done looking at it. You can read the manual page by adding the "man" directory to your MANPATH and running "man tlearn": % setenv MANPATH "${MANPATH}:/share/instsw

": % setenv MANPATH "${MANPATH}:/share/instsww/pkg/tlearn/man" % man tlearn or, equivalently: % env MANPATH=/share/instsww/pkg/tlearn/man man tlearn To read the xtlearn manual page use "man xtlearn". You can put the "setenv"

e use "man xtlearn". You can put the "setenv" command at the end of your ~/.cshrc file if you plan to read the manual page a lot; once you do that, and log in again, you need only type "man tlearn" to read the page. You can read the docum

earn" to read the page. You can read the documentation for the CONNEX graphics input files using the command % more /share/instsww/pkg/tlearn/src/MozerLib/doc/connex.doc Tlearn installation files (UNIX) -------------------------------

ion files (UNIX) ------------------------------- The following files have been installed for 'tlearn': tlearn/bin/tlearn <-- multi-architecture wrapper tlearn/bin/tlearn_dsp <-- " " " tlearn/bin/xtlearn
<-- " " " tlearn/bin/xtlearn <-- " " " tlearn/bin/HP-UX/tlearn <-- tlearn program itself (HP) tlearn/bin/HP-UX/tlearn_dsp <-- CONNEX display version (HP) tlearn/bin/HP-UX/xtlearn

play version (HP) tlearn/bin/HP-UX/xtlearn <-- X-Window interface program (HP) tlearn/man/man1/tlearn.1 <-- man page for tlearn tlearn/man/man1/xtlearn.1 <-- man page for xtlearn tlearn/Exp/exp.c tlearn/Exp/exp.o tlear

n tlearn/Exp/exp.c tlearn/Exp/exp.o tlearn/Exp/Makefile tlearn/Exp/exp_table.hp <-- exponent lookup table tlearn/src <-- directory with source, test input files Tlearn on the Instructional NT systems --------

Tlearn on the Instructional NT systems -------------------------------------- 'tlearn' is available on the Instructional NT systems: Run: \\fileservice\cs188\tlearn\Tlearn.exe (32-bit MS Windows program) Run: \\fileservice\cs188\tlea

Windows program) Run: \\fileservice\cs188\tlearn\TlearnManual.pdf (docs in 'acroread' format) Also see \\fileservice\Software\Tlearn Folder. Reporting bugs -------------- Please report bugs in the Instructional installation to IESG staff

gs in the Instructional installation to IESG staff at . We will coordinate with the maintainers at UCSD, reachable at , to get bugs fixed. For more information -------------------- For more information, visit the following URLs in your favorite

rmation, visit the following URLs in your favorite web browser: http://crl.ucsd.edu/innate/tlearn.html ftp://ftp.crl.ucsd.edu/public/neuralnets/tlearn ftp://ftp.crl.ucsd.edu/public/neuralnets/tlearn/README EECS Instructional Support
